meta.title: Range Rover 4.4 TDV8 SDV8 Engine Replacement Cost UK
meta.description: Every Range Rover 4.4 V8 diesel engine — 448DT. L322 and L405 generations, real UK costs and reliability. Get free quotes from 12 vetted Land Rover specialists today.

@graph.name: Land Rover Range Rover 4.4 TDV8 / SDV8 Engine Era Map
Land Rover Range Rover 4.4 TDV8 / SDV8 Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Range Rover 4.4 TDV8 / SDV8 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 448DT engine typically costs £6,500–£10,500 supply only depending on generation (L322 vs L405). Fitting adds £1,100–£2,500. Main dealer new engines can exceed £24,000. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the Range Rover 4.4 TDV8 / SDV8 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 448DT is widely considered the most reliable engine in the JLR diesel lineup — a genuine step up from the problematic 3.0 TDV6/SDV6. It's rated ★★★★☆, but has known maintenance items: oil cooler leaks, oil dilution on L405, and early L322 turbo oil drain pipe issues. No catastrophic bottom-end design flaws.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Range Rover 4.4 TDV8 / SDV8?

acceptedAnswer.text: Oil cooler leaks (all years), oil dilution from DPF regeneration (L405 SDV8), turbo oil drain pipe issues (early L322 2010–2012), drive belt tensioner failure, and brittle air intake pipes. Timing chain wear is occasionally reported on higher-mileage examples.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Range Rover 4.4 TDV8 / SDV8 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Against a £15,000–£30,000 L405, a £6,500–£10,500 recon engine is a significant but worthwhile investment given the Range Rover's strong retained value. For an L322 worth £5,000–£10,000, carefully assess the rest of the vehicle before committing. Replacement is almost always viable for well-maintained examples.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Range Rover's engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: For the 4.4 TDV8/SDV8, the engine code is 448DT — it's on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The badge differs by generation: L322 (2010–2012) is badged '4.4 TDV8', while L405 (2012–2021) is badged '4.4 SDV8'. If you're unsure, tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the 448DT's oil cooler, or does it need a new engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the oil cooler leak is a straightforward repair costing £800–£1,500. The leak typically comes from gaskets and O-rings on the turbo oil feed pipe. If left too long, cross-contamination can cause premature wear, but the engine itself is not normally replaced for this issue alone.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Range Rover 4.4 TDV8 / SDV8 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Straightforward gasket and O-ring replacement; catch it before cross-contamination
If caught before bearing damage; if not, engine replacement required
Preventative — replace the pipe before it kills the turbo
Simple replacement; don't let it throw the belt
If caught early; chain replacement is a big job but far cheaper than a new engine
Not a common 448DT failure; if it occurs, replacement is the only option

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 4.4 TDV8 / SDV8? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with documented oil changes — this engine is unforgiving of neglect
* L405 with proof of frequent oil changes (every 5,000–7,000 miles) to mitigate oil dilution
* Oil cooler gaskets replaced as a preventative measure
* L322 2010–2012 with evidence of turbo oil drain pipe work
avoidIf: * Service history shows only urban driving patterns (DPF and oil dilution risk)
* Oil level is visibly above the max mark on the dipstick — diesel contamination
* Any evidence of oil in coolant or coolant in oil — oil cooler failure
* Engine warning light present with no diagnosis documentation

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 4.4 TDV8/SDV8 is widely considered the most reliable engine in the JLR diesel lineup — a genuine step up from the problematic 3.0 TDV6/SDV6. But it's a complex V8 diesel with known maintenance items: oil cooler leaks, oil dilution on the L405, and early L322 turbo oil drain pipe issues. Buy on service history and proof of preventative maintenance. If you're replacing, a reconditioned unit with a 12-month warranty is the sweet spot — dealer new engines can exceed £24,000.

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover 4.4 TDV8 / SDV8 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any timing chain rattle or clatter; the 448DT should start smoothly
* Service History — Including which generation (L322 2010–2012 vs L405 2012–2021) — see the Era Map above
* Oil Level Check — Is the oil above the max mark? This indicates potential diesel contamination from oil dilution
* Coolant Condition — Is there oil in the coolant? Oil cooler leak indicator
* Oil Leaks — Check the valley between cylinder banks for oil pooling (oil cooler leak)
* Turbo Health — Any blue smoke, whine, or increased oil consumption? Turbo oil drain pipe issue on early cars
* DPF Regeneration — Has the vehicle had regular motorway runs? Short journeys accelerate DPF and oil dilution issues
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures; often point to DPF or EGR problems
